statute: M.G.L. Chapter 142A (Home Improvement Contractor Registration); M.G.L. Chapter 149, § 44F (Construction Supervisor License)
license_label: MA Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) Registration Number / Construction Supervisor License (CSL) Number
license_notes: Home improvement contractors must register with the HIC Program for owner-occupied 1-4 family projects. CSL required for all residential construction on 1-2 family homes. Penalty: $5,000 fine and up to 2 years in prison for unlicensed work.

mandatory_clauses.label: MA HIC / CSL disclosure
3-day right to cancel
mandatory_clauses.text:
RIGHT TO CANCEL: You may cancel this contract without penalty within three business days of signing. To cancel, send written notice to:
{{contractor_name}}, {{contractor_address}}

mandatory_clauses.label: Arbitration notice (if applicable)
mandatory_clauses.text: NOTE: If this contract contains an arbitration clause, disputes may be resolved through arbitration rather than court. Review any arbitration provisions carefully before signing.

mandatory_clauses.label: Home Improvement Guaranty Fund notice
mandatory_clauses.text: GUARANTY FUND NOTICE: The Massachusetts Home Improvement Contractor Program maintains a Guaranty Fund to compensate homeowners for losses resulting from HIC violations. For information, contact the MA Office of Consumer Affairs and Business Regulation.

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: $5,000 fine + up to 2 years prison for unlicensed work. Contract void if contractor unregistered. No specific license requirement for MA lien rights.
notes: Massachusetts requires both HIC registration (for home improvement work on owner-occupied 1-4 family properties) and a Construction Supervisor License (CSL) for new residential construction on 1-2 family homes. Written contract required for jobs over $500. HIC registration number and CSL number should both appear on contracts. 3-day cancellation right.
